Parameters:

```json
{
  "plan": "# Ticker 24h Change via Kline + Multi-line Display\n\n## Approach\n\nThe &#96;MarketData&#96; struct from the API does not return a native 24h change field (the &#96;change_24h_percent&#96; alias is never populated by the server). We will fetch a single daily kline (resolution &#96;1D&#96;) per symbol to get the open price of the current day, then compute &#96;change = (last_price - open) / open * 100&#96;.\n\nThis kline fetch will be done in parallel alongside the existing market data fetch, using &#96;join_all&#96;.\n\n## Changes\n\n### 1. Fetch 24h kline data in [src/commands.rs](src/commands.rs)\n\nIn &#96;fetch_and_display_dashboard&#96;, after building &#96;symbol_list&#96;, add a parallel kline fetch for each symbol:\n\n- For each symbol, call &#96;client.get_kline(symbol, \"1D\", from, to)&#96; where &#96;from = now - 86400&#96; and &#96;to = now&#96;.\n- Run all kline fetches via &#96;join_all&#96; in parallel with the existing &#96;get_symbol_market&#96; calls.\n- Build a &#96;HashMap&lt;String, f64&gt;&#96; mapping symbol -&gt; 24h change percentage.\n- After collecting &#96;market: Vec&lt;MarketData&gt;&#96;, populate &#96;change_24h_percent&#96; on each &#96;MarketData&#96; from the kline-derived map.\n\nThe parallel fetch block (around line 995) becomes:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;rust\nlet now_ts = chrono::Utc::now().timestamp();\nlet from_ts = now_ts - 86400;\n\nlet (market_results, kline_results) = tokio::join!(\n    join_all(symbol_list.iter().map(|s| client.get_symbol_market(s))),\n    join_all(symbol_list.iter().map(|s| client.get_kline(s, \"1D\", from_ts, now_ts)))\n);\n\nlet mut market: Vec&lt;MarketData&gt; = market_results\n    .into_iter()\n    .filter_map(std::result::Result::ok)\n    .collect();\n\n// Build symbol -&gt; open_price map from kline data\nlet mut open_prices: std::collections::HashMap&lt;String, f64&gt; = std::collections::HashMap::new();\nfor (i, result) in kline_results.into_iter().enumerate() {\n    if let Ok(klines) = result {\n        if let Some(k) = klines.first() {\n            if let Ok(open) = k.open.parse::&lt;f64&gt;() {\n                open_prices.insert(symbol_list[i].clone(), open);\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n\n// Populate change_24h_percent from kline open price\nfor m in &amp;mut market {\n    if m.change_24h_percent.is_empty() || m.change_24h_percent == \"0\" {\n        if let Some(open) = open_prices.get(&amp;m.symbol) {\n            if *open &gt; 0.0 {\n                if let Ok(last) = m.last_price.parse::&lt;f64&gt;() {\n                    let change = ((last - open) / open) * 100.0;\n                    m.change_24h_percent = format!(\"{:.2}\", change);\n                }\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### 2. Display tickers as one-per-line in [src/output.rs](src/output.rs)\n\nChange the TICKERS section (around line 260-290) from joining all tickers with &#96;\" | \"&#96; into a single line, to displaying each ticker on its own line inside the box:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;rust\n// TICKERS\npush_line(&amp;mut output, \" TICKERS:\");\nif snapshot.market.is_empty() {\n    push_line(&amp;mut output, \"   No market data\");\n} else {\n    for m in &amp;snapshot.market {\n        let change_display = m\n            .change_24h_percent\n            .parse::&lt;f64&gt;()\n            .ok()\n            .map(|change| {\n                let arrow = if change &gt; 0.0 { \"в–І\" } else if change &lt; 0.0 { \"в–ј\" } else { \"\" };\n                format!(\"{}{:.2}%\", arrow, change.abs())\n            })\n            .unwrap_or_else(|| \"N/A\".to_string());\n        push_line(&amp;mut output, &amp;format!(\"   {} ${} {}\", m.symbol, m.mark_price, change_display));\n    }\n}\noutput.push_str(&amp;sep());\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Expected Output\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\nв”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө\nв”Ӯ TICKERS:                                                                    в”Ӯ\nв”Ӯ   BTC-USD $72797.35 в–І1.23%                                                 в”Ӯ\nв”Ӯ   ETH-USD $2120.50 в–ј0.88%                                                  в”Ӯ\nв”Ӯ   XAG-USD $84.36 в–І0.45%                                                    в”Ӯ\nв”ңв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Җв”Ө\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Files Modified\n\n- [src/commands.rs](src/commands.rs) -- add kline fetch, populate &#96;change_24h_percent&#96;\n- [src/output.rs](src/output.rs) -- change TICKERS to multi-line display\n",
```

Parameters:

```json
{
  "plan": "# WebSocket Trade Streaming in Dashboard Watch Mode\n\n## Current Behavior\n\nIn watch mode, every N seconds the dashboard calls &#96;client.get_recent_trades(first_symbol, Some(5))&#96; via REST to get the latest 5 trades. This means trades are up to N seconds stale and each refresh adds an extra HTTP round-trip.\n\n## Proposed Change\n\nWhen &#96;--watch&#96; is active:\n1. Start a &#96;public_trade&#96; WebSocket subscription for the first symbol before entering the watch loop.\n2. Maintain a shared &#96;Arc&lt;RwLock&lt;VecDeque&lt;Trade&gt;&gt;&gt;&#96; buffer (capped at 5 entries) that the WebSocket task continuously updates.\n3. On each refresh, the dashboard reads trades from this shared buffer instead of calling the REST API.\n4. When watch mode exits (Ctrl+C), the WebSocket connection is dropped automatically.\n5. In non-watch mode (single snapshot), continue using REST as before.\n\n## Architecture\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart TD\n    start[handle_dashboard]\n    start --&gt;|watch=None| singleShot[REST fetch all + print]\n    start --&gt;|\"watch=Some(N)\"| wsSetup[\"Start WebSocket public_trade subscription\"]\n    wsSetup --&gt; spawnWs[\"tokio::spawn: WS receiver -&gt; Arc trades buffer\"]\n    spawnWs --&gt; restSeed[\"REST get_recent_trades for initial seed\"]\n    restSeed --&gt; watchLoop[Watch Loop]\n    watchLoop --&gt; buildFrame[\"build_dashboard_output(trades from Arc buffer)\"]\n    buildFrame --&gt; render[\"Clear screen + print\"]\n    render --&gt; selectWait[\"tokio::select! sleep(N) vs ctrl_c\"]\n    selectWait --&gt;|timeout| watchLoop\n    selectWait --&gt;|ctrl_c| exitWatch[Exit]\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n## Implementation in [src/commands.rs](src/commands.rs)\n\n### Step 1: Add shared trades buffer type\n\nNear the top of the file, add:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;rust\nuse std::collections::VecDeque;\nuse std::sync::Arc;\nuse tokio::sync::RwLock;\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Step 2: Modify &#96;handle_dashboard&#96; to start WebSocket when watching\n\nWhen &#96;watch.is_some()&#96; and there is a first symbol, create the WebSocket and spawn a background task:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;rust\nlet trades_buffer: Arc&lt;RwLock&lt;VecDeque&lt;Trade&gt;&gt;&gt; = Arc::new(RwLock::new(VecDeque::new()));\n\nif watch.is_some() {\n    if let Some(first_symbol) = symbol_list.first() {\n        // Seed with initial REST data\n        if let Ok(initial_trades) = client.get_recent_trades(first_symbol, Some(5)).await {\n            let mut buf = trades_buffer.write().await;\n            for t in initial_trades {\n                buf.push_back(t);\n            }\n        }\n        // Start WebSocket stream\n        if let Ok(ws) = StandXWebSocket::without_auth() {\n            let _ = ws.subscribe(\"public_trade\", Some(first_symbol)).await;\n            if let Ok(mut rx) = ws.connect().await {\n                let buf = trades_buffer.clone();\n                tokio::spawn(async move {\n                    while let Some(msg) = rx.recv().await {\n                        if let WsMessage::Trade(trade) = msg {\n                            let mut trades = buf.write().await;\n                            trades.push_front(trade);\n                            while trades.len() &gt; 5 {\n                                trades.pop_back();\n                            }\n                        }\n                    }\n                });\n            }\n        }\n    }\n}\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n### Step 3: Pass the shared buffer to &#96;build_dashboard_output&#96;\n\nAdd a parameter &#96;ws_trades: Option&lt;Arc&lt;RwLock&lt;VecDeque&lt;Trade&gt;&gt;&gt;&gt;&#96; to &#96;build_dashboard_output&#96;. When this parameter is &#96;Some&#96;:\n- Read from the buffer (&#96;ws_trades.read().await&#96;) to get trades.\n- Skip the REST &#96;get_recent_trades&#96; call.\n\nWhen &#96;None&#96; (non-watch single-shot mode), continue using REST as before.\n\n### Step 4: Read from buffer in build function\n\nIn &#96;build_dashboard_output&#96;, around the trades fetch block (currently ~line 1048):\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;rust\nlet (trades, order_book) = if let Some(first_symbol) = symbol_list.first() {\n    let trades = if let Some(ref ws_buf) = ws_trades {\n        let buf = ws_buf.read().await;\n        buf.iter().cloned().collect()\n    } else {\n        client.get_recent_trades(first_symbol, Some(5)).await.unwrap_or_default()\n    };\n    let order_book_result = client.get_depth(first_symbol, Some(5)).await;\n    (trades, order_book_result.ok())\n} else {\n    (Vec::new(), None)\n};\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nNote: the &#96;get_depth&#96; call stays as REST since the WebSocket depth channel pushes full snapshots too frequently and the dashboard only needs a static snapshot per refresh.\n\n## Files Modified\n\n- [src/commands.rs](src/commands.rs) -- WebSocket setup in watch mode, shared buffer, pass to build function\n",
  "name": "WebSocket trade streaming in dashboard",
  "todos": [
    {

    }
  ],
  "overview": "When dashboard runs in &#96;--watch&#96; mode, use a WebSocket stream (&#96;public_trade&#96; channel) for the first symbol's recent trades instead of polling the REST API each refresh cycle. Other panels (account, positions, orders, tickers, depth) continue polling on the interval."
}
```

```
+use standx_cli::models::{
+    DashboardSnapshot, OrderSide, OrderType, PortfolioSnapshot, TimeInForce, Trade,
+};
 use standx_cli::output;
 use standx_cli::websocket::{StandXWebSocket, WsMessage};
-use std::collections::HashMap;
+use std::collections::{HashMap, VecDeque};
+use std::sync::Arc;
 use futures::future::join_all;
 use std::future::Future;
 use std::time::Duration;
 use tokio::signal;
+use tokio::sync::RwLock;
 
 /// Portfolio command for direct execution (without subcommands)
 #[derive(Debug)]
@@ -896,10 +900,67 @@ pub async fn handle_dashboard(
         vec![]
     };
     let client = StandXClient::new()?;
+    let ws_trades: Arc&lt;RwLock&lt;VecDeque&lt;Trade&gt;&gt;&gt; = Arc::new(RwLock::new(VecDeque::new()));
+
+    if watch.is_some() {
+        let first_symbol = if let Some(symbol) = symbol_list.first() {
+            Some(symbol.clone())
+        } else {
+            client
+                .get_symbol_info()
+                .await
+                .ok()
+                .and_then(|symbols| symbols.into_iter().next().map(|s| s.symbol))
+        };
+
+        if let Some(first_symbol) = first_symbol {
+            // Seed initial trades so first frame has data even before websocket receives updates.
+            if let Ok(initial_trades) = client.get_recent_trades(&amp;first_symbol, Some(5)).await {
+                let mut buf = ws_trades.write().await;
+                for trade in initial_trades {
+                    buf.push_back(trade);
+                }
+                while buf.len() &gt; 5 {
+                    buf.pop_back();
+                }
+            }
+
+            if let Ok(ws) = StandXWebSocket::without_auth() {
+                let _ = ws.subscribe("public_trade", Some(&amp;first_symbol)).await;
+                if let Ok(mut rx) = ws.connect().await {
+                    let ws_trades_clone = ws_trades.clone();
+                    tokio::spawn(async move {
+                        while let Some(msg) = rx.recv().await {
+                            if let WsMessage::Trade(trade) = msg {
+                                let mut trades = ws_trades_clone.write().await;
+                                trades.push_front(trade);
+                                while trades.len() &gt; 5 {
+                                    trades.pop_back();
+                                }
+                            }
+                        }
+                    });
+                }
+            }
+        }
+    }
 
     run_watch_loop(
         watch,
-        || build_dashboard_output(&amp;client, &amp;symbol_list, verbose, output_format, compact),
+        || {
+            build_dashboard_output(
+                &amp;client,
+                &amp;symbol_list,
+                verbose,
+                output_format,
+                compact,
+                if watch.is_some() {
+                    Some(ws_trades.clone())
+                } else {
+                    None
+                },
+            )
+        },
         "Dashboard refresh failed",
     )
     .await
@@ -912,6 +973,7 @@ async fn build_dashboard_output(
     _verbose: bool,
     output_format: OutputFormat,
     compact: bool,
+    ws_trades: Option&lt;Arc&lt;RwLock&lt;VecDeque&lt;Trade&gt;&gt;&gt;&gt;,
 ) -&gt; Result&lt;String&gt; {
     // Check if filtering by symbols
     let has_filter = !symbol_filter.is_empty();
@@ -1039,16 +1101,21 @@ async fn build_dashboard_output(
         }
     }
 
-    // Fetch recent trades + order book for first symbol in parallel
+    // Fetch recent trades + order book for first symbol.
+    // In watch mode we prefer websocket-fed trades buffer to avoid polling for trades.
     let (trades, order_book) = if let Some(first_symbol) = symbol_list.first() {
-        let (trades_result, order_book_result) = tokio::join!(
-            client.get_recent_trades(first_symbol, Some(5)),
-            client.get_depth(first_symbol, Some(5))
-        );
-        (
-            trades_result.unwrap_or_default(),
-            order_book_result.ok(),
-        )
+        let trades = if let Some(ws_buf) = ws_trades {
+            let buf = ws_buf.read().await;
+            buf.iter().cloned().collect()
+        } else {
+            client
+                .get_recent_trades(first_symbol, Some(5))
+                .await
+                .unwrap_or_default()
+        };
+
+        let order_book = client.get_depth(first_symbol, Some(5)).await.ok();
+        (trades, order_book)
     } else {
         (Vec::new(), None)
     };
```

е·ІжҢү plan е®ҢжҲҗе®һзҺ°пјҢдё”дёӨдёӘ todo йғҪе·Ід»Һ `in_progress` жҺЁиҝӣеҲ° `completed`гҖӮ

дё»иҰҒж”№еҠЁеңЁ `src/commands.rs`пјҡ

- еңЁ `handle_dashboard` дёӯж–°еўһе…ұдә«зј“еӯҳпјҡ`Arc<RwLock<VecDeque<Trade>>>`пјҲе®№йҮҸдёҠйҷҗ 5пјүгҖӮ
- `--watch` жЁЎејҸдёӢдјҡпјҡ
  - зЎ®е®ҡйҰ–дёӘ symbolпјҲдјҳе…Ҳз”ЁжҲ·иҝҮж»ӨеҲ—иЎЁйҰ–йЎ№пјӣиӢҘжңӘдј  `--symbol(s)`пјҢе°қиҜ•д»Һ `get_symbol_info()` еҸ–з¬¬дёҖдёӘпјүгҖӮ
  - е…Ҳз”Ё REST `get_recent_trades(..., Some(5))` еҒҡдёҖж¬Ў seedгҖӮ
  - еҗҜеҠЁ `StandXWebSocket::without_auth()`пјҢи®ўйҳ… `public_trade`пјҢеҗҺеҸ° `tokio::spawn` жҢҒз»ӯеҶҷе…Ҙзј“еӯҳпјҲж–° trade `push_front`пјҢи¶… 5 жқЎ `pop_back`пјүгҖӮ
- `build_dashboard_output` ж–°еўһеҸӮж•° `ws_trades: Option<Arc<RwLock<VecDeque<Trade>>>>`пјҡ
  - `Some(...)` ж—¶д»Һзј“еӯҳиҜ» tradesпјҢдёҚеҶҚиҪ®иҜў REST tradesгҖӮ
  - `None`пјҲеҚ•ж¬Ўеҝ«з…§пјүж—¶дҝқжҢҒеҺҹйҖ»иҫ‘пјҢз»§з»ӯ REST `get_recent_trades`гҖӮ
  - `order_book` д»ҚдҝқжҢҒ REST `get_depth`пјҲдёҺ plan дёҖиҮҙпјүгҖӮ

йӘҢиҜҒз»“жһңпјҡ

- `cargo check -q` йҖҡиҝҮгҖӮ
- `cargo test -q test_parse_relative_time_hours` йҖҡиҝҮпјҲжңүдёҖдәӣйЎ№зӣ®еҶ…ж—ўжңү warningпјҢдҪҶдёҺиҝҷж¬Ўж”№еҠЁж— е…іпјүгҖӮ
- `ReadLints` жЈҖжҹҘ `src/commands.rs` ж— ж–°еўһ lint й”ҷиҜҜгҖӮ

еҰӮжһңдҪ ж„ҝж„ҸпјҢжҲ‘еҸҜд»ҘеҶҚиЎҘдёҖдёӘе°Ҹзҡ„еӣһеҪ’жөӢиҜ•пјҲmock WS зј“еҶІиҜ»еҸ–и·Ҝеҫ„пјүпјҢиҰҶзӣ– watch жЁЎејҸдёӢ trades жқҘжәҗеҲҮжҚўйҖ»иҫ‘гҖӮ

```
+    let ws_trades: Arc&lt;RwLock&lt;VecDeque&lt;Trade&gt;&gt;&gt; = Arc::new(RwLock::new(VecDeque::new()));
+    let mut ws_trade_updates_rx: Option&lt;watch::Receiver&lt;u64&gt;&gt; = None;
+    let mut ws_trades_enabled = false;
+
+    if watch.is_some() {
+        let first_symbol = if let Some(symbol) = symbol_list.first() {
+            Some(symbol.clone())
+        } else {
+            client
+                .get_symbol_info()
+                .await
+                .ok()
+                .and_then(|symbols| symbols.into_iter().next().map(|s| s.symbol))
+        };
+
+        if let Some(first_symbol) = first_symbol {
+            // Seed initial trades so first frame has data even before websocket receives updates.
+            if let Ok(initial_trades) = client.get_recent_trades(&amp;first_symbol, Some(5)).await {
+                let mut buf = ws_trades.write().await;
+                for trade in initial_trades {
+                    buf.push_back(trade);
+                }
+                while buf.len() &gt; 5 {
+                    buf.pop_back();
+                }
+            }
+
+            if let Ok(ws) = StandXWebSocket::without_auth() {
+                if ws.subscribe("public_trade", Some(&amp;first_symbol)).await.is_ok() {
+                    let (trade_updates_tx, trade_updates_rx) = watch::channel(0_u64);
+                    ws_trade_updates_rx = Some(trade_updates_rx);
+                    let mut update_seq: u64 = 0;
+                    if let Ok(mut rx) = ws.connect().await {
+                        ws_trades_enabled = true;
+                    let ws_trades_clone = ws_trades.clone();
+                    tokio::spawn(async move {
+                        while let Some(msg) = rx.recv().await {
+                            if let WsMessage::Trade(trade) = msg {
+                                let mut trades = ws_trades_clone.write().await;
+                                trades.push_front(trade);
+                                while trades.len() &gt; 5 {
+                                    trades.pop_back();
+                                }
+                                update_seq = update_seq.wrapping_add(1);
+                                let _ = trade_updates_tx.send(update_seq);
+                            }
+                        }
+                    });
+                    }
+                }
+            }
+        }
+    }

@@ -1,8 +1,46 @@
 //! Output formatting utilities
 
 use crate::models::*;
+use chrono::{DateTime, TimeZone, Utc};
 use tabled::{Table as TabledTable, Tabled};
 
+fn format_trade_time_short(raw: &amp;str) -&gt; String {
+    if raw.is_empty() {
+        return String::new();
+    }
+
+    // Handle unix timestamps from API/websocket (seconds or milliseconds).
+    if let Ok(ts) = raw.parse::&lt;i64&gt;() {
+        let dt = if raw.len() &gt;= 13 {
+            Utc.timestamp_millis_opt(ts).single()
+        } else {
+            Utc.timestamp_opt(ts, 0).single()
+        };
+        if let Some(dt) = dt {
+            return dt.format("%H:%M:%S").to_string();
+        }
+    }
+
+    // Handle RFC3339-like strings: "2026-03-04T02:21:26.633550Z"
+    if let Ok(dt) = DateTime::parse_from_rfc3339(raw) {
+        return dt.with_timezone(&amp;Utc).format("%H:%M:%S").to_string();
+    }
+
+    // Fallback to the previous best-effort splitter.
+    if raw.contains('T') {
+        return raw
+            .split('T')
+            .nth(1)
+            .unwrap_or(raw)
+            .split('.')
+            .next()
+            .unwrap_or(raw)
+            .to_string();
+    }
+
+    raw.to_string()
+}
+
 /// Format data as table
 pub fn format_table&lt;T: Tabled&gt;(data: Vec&lt;T&gt;) -&gt; String {
     TabledTable::new(data).to_string()
@@ -399,18 +437,7 @@ pub fn format_dashboard_mvp(snapshot: &amp;DashboardSnapshot, compact: bool) -&gt; Stri
             push_line(&amp;mut output, "   No recent trades");
         } else {
             for t in &amp;snapshot.trades {
-                // Format time to HH:MM:SS from ISO format "2026-03-04T02:21:26.633550Z"
-                let time_short = if t.time.contains('T') {
-                    t.time
-                        .split('T')
-                        .nth(1)
-                        .unwrap_or(&amp;t.time)
-                        .split('.')
-                        .next()
-                        .unwrap_or(&amp;t.time)
-                } else {
-                    &amp;t.time
-                };
+                let time_short = format_trade_time_short(&amp;t.time);
                 // Use is_buyer_taker to determine side
```

е·ІеӨ„зҗҶиҝҷдёӨдёӘзӮ№пјҢе№¶дё”йҖҡиҝҮдәҶзј–иҜ‘жЈҖжҹҘгҖӮ

### ж”№еҠЁз»“жһң

- **Trade ж—¶й—ҙжҲіи§Јжһҗе·Ідҝ®еӨҚ**пјҲ`src/output.rs`пјү
  - ж–°еўһ `format_trade_time_short()`пјҢж”ҜжҢҒпјҡ
    - Unix **жҜ«з§’**ж—¶й—ҙжҲіпјҲеҰӮ `1772639418307`пјү
    - Unix **з§’**ж—¶й—ҙжҲі
    - RFC3339 ж—¶й—ҙдёІпјҲеҰӮ `2026-03-04T02:21:26.633550Z`пјү
  - `RECENT TRADES` жёІжҹ“ж”№дёәз»ҹдёҖиө°иҜҘеҮҪж•°пјҢеұ•зӨәдёә `HH:MM:SS`гҖӮ

- **Watch жЁЎејҸж”ҜжҢҒвҖңжңүж–° trade еҚіеҲ·ж–°вҖқ**пјҲ`src/commands.rs`пјү
  - еңЁ WS ж”¶еҲ° `WsMessage::Trade` ж—¶пјҢйҖҡиҝҮ `tokio::sync::watch` еҸ‘еҮәжӣҙж–°дҝЎеҸ·гҖӮ
  - `run_watch_loop` зҺ°еңЁеңЁ `tokio::select!` дёӯеҗҢж—¶зӯүеҫ…пјҡ
    - `ctrl_c`
    - еӣәе®ҡй—ҙйҡ” `sleep(--watch)`
    - **WS trade жӣҙж–°дҝЎеҸ·**
  - иҝҷж ·дёҚдјҡеҸӘзӯүеӣәе®ҡй—ҙйҡ”пјҢжқҘж–° trade ж—¶дјҡжҸҗеүҚеҲ·ж–°пјӣеӣәе®ҡй—ҙйҡ”д»Қдҝқз•ҷдёәе…ңеә•еҲ·ж–°гҖӮ

- **е…је®№жҖ§еӨ„зҗҶ**
  - еҸӘжңү WS жҲҗеҠҹиҝһжҺҘж—¶жүҚеҗҜз”Ё `ws_trades` зј“еҶІиҜ»еҸ–пјӣеҗҰеҲҷиҮӘеҠЁеӣһиҗҪ REST tradesпјҢйҒҝе…Қз©әеҲ—иЎЁеҚЎдҪҸгҖӮ

### йӘҢиҜҒ

- `cargo check -q`пјҡйҖҡиҝҮ
- `ReadLints`пјҲ`src/commands.rs`гҖҒ`src/output.rs`пјүпјҡж— ж–°еўһ lint й”ҷиҜҜ

еҰӮжһңдҪ еёҢжңӣпјҢжҲ‘еҸҜд»ҘеҶҚеҠ дёҖдёӘ**жңҖе°ҸеҲ·ж–°й—ҙйҡ”иҠӮжөҒ**пјҲдҫӢеҰӮ 200~500msпјүпјҢйҒҝе…Қй«ҳйў‘дәӨжҳ“еҜ№ж—¶и§ҰеҸ‘иҝҮдәҺеҜҶйӣҶзҡ„ж•ҙеұҸйҮҚз»ҳгҖӮ  
еҸҰеӨ–еҪ“еүҚе®һзҺ°е’Ң Issue #156 зҡ„ж•°жҚ®жқҘжәҗе®ҡд№үжҳҜдёҖиҮҙж–№еҗ‘пјҲRECENT TRADES жқҘиҮӘеёӮеңәдәӨжҳ“пјүпјҡ[Issue #156](https://github.com/wjllance/standx-cli/issues/156)гҖӮ

Now I have a clear picture of the current layout. Let me also check the Issue #156 spec for the intended 2-column layout.

Issue #156 already shows this layout in the spec:

```
в”Ӯ  ORDER BOOK (BTCUSDT):                    ACTIVE ORDERS:              в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Asks: 67895(2.5) 67890(5.0) 67888(1.2)    #1 BUY 0.1 @65,000        в”Ӯ
в”Ӯ  Bids: 67885(3.2) 67880(8.1) 67875(1.9)    #2 SELL 0.2 @70,000       в”Ӯ
```

So the spec envisions ORDER BOOK + ACTIVE ORDERS side by side. But since we've moved POSITIONS/ACTIVE ORDERS to the bottom, you're asking about ORDER BOOK + RECENT TRADES side by side. Let me create a plan.

---

is a visual column separator.\n\n## Implementation\n\nAll changes in [src/output.rs](src/output.rs), in the &#96;format_dashboard_mvp&#96; function.\n\n### Step 1 -- Add a &#96;push_two_col&#96; helper\n\nA new closure (similar to &#96;push_line&#96;) that takes a left string and right string, truncates/pads each to its half-width, and joins them with a &#96;в”Ӯ&#96; separator:\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;rust\nlet left_w = (width - 1) / 2;   // -1 for the middle в”Ӯ\nlet right_w = width - 1 - left_w;\nlet push_two_col = |out: &amp;mut String, left: &amp;str, right: &amp;str| {\n    let l = truncate_pad(left, left_w);\n    let r = truncate_pad(right, right_w);\n    out.push_str(&amp;format!(\"в”Ӯ{}в”Ӯ{}в”Ӯ\\n\", l, r));\n};\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\nWhere &#96;truncate_pad&#96; is a small helper that truncates to max chars then pads with spaces (same logic as the existing &#96;fit&#96; closure but parameterized on width).\n\n### Step 2 -- Build left/right line vectors\n\nBefore rendering, collect the ORDER BOOK lines and the RECENT TRADES lines as two &#96;Vec&lt;String&gt;&#96;:\n\n- **Left (ORDER BOOK):** title line, Asks line, Bids line (or \"unavailable\")\n- **Right (RECENT TRADES):** title line, up to 5 trade lines (or \"No recent trades\")\n\n### Step 3 -- Render side by side\n\nZip the two vectors by index up to &#96;max(left.len(), right.len())&#96;, padding the shorter side with empty strings. Use &#96;push_two_col&#96; for each row.\n\nReplace the current separate ORDER BOOK section + RECENT TRADES section with this single combined block (between &#96;sep()&#96; lines).\n\n### Step 4 -- Handle &#96;--compact&#96; mode\n\nWhen &#96;compact&#96; is true, the right column (RECENT TRADES) produces no lines. Fall back to the existing full-width &#96;push_line&#96; rendering for ORDER BOOK alone, keeping the current behavior.\n\n## Visual Width Considerations\n\nWith the default &#96;width = 78&#96;, each column gets ~38 chars of inner content. This is enough for:\n- ORDER BOOK: &#96;\" Asks: 72835(0.07) 72837(0.01)\"&#96; fits in 38 chars\n- RECENT TRADES: &#96;\" 00:12:38 72846.37 0.0056 SELL\"&#96; fits in 38 chars\n\nIf the terminal is narrow (width &lt; 66), the 2-column layout would be too cramped, so we should fall back to the stacked single-column layout.\n",
